content.claim: A minority-language school bus program transports more than 500 Hungarian-minority students across 13 Transylvanian counties using vehicles seating 8–21 people, demonstrating a multi-village, route-based transport logistics model; direct applicability to domestic Hungarian rural consolidation is inferred but not confirmed.
content.context: Fresh live research through the School network planning lens
limitations: Cross-border context (Romania) differs from domestic Hungarian administrative structure
Ethnic-minority funding and legal framework not directly transferable to rural Hungarian village consolidation
Vehicle scale and routing model inferred as relevant rather than empirically tested for Hungarian domestic application
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
